1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110

115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.

121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.

127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.

135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).

143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.

145

62	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
63	
64	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
65	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
66	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
67	
68	namespace PresheafOfModules
69	
70	open InternalHom Opposite
71	
72	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
73	
74	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
75	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
76	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
77	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
78	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
79	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
80	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
81	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
82	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
83	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
84	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
85	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
86	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
87	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
89	  exact
90	    { toFun := fun φ =>
91	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
92	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
93	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
94	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
95	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
96	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
97	      invFun := fun r =>
98	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
99	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
100	      left_inv := fun φ => by
101	        ext Y
102	        dsimp only
103	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
104	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
105	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
106	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
107	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
108	        rfl
109	      right_inv := fun r => by
110	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
111	            (restr X.unop
112	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
113	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
114	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
115	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
116	        exact mul_one r }
117	
118	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
119	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
120	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
121	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
122	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
123	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
124	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
125	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
126	    (fun {X Y} f => by
127	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
128	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
129	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
130	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
131	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
132	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
133	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
134	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
135	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
136	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
137	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
138	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
139	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
140	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
141	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
142	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
143	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
144	      rw [hrm] at key
145	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
146	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
147	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
148	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
149	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
150	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
151	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
152	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
153	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
154	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
155	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
156	
157	end PresheafOfModules

165	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
166	
167	open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
168	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
169	
170	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
171	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
172	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
173	```
174	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
175	```
176	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
177	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
178

28	### Decision: how to obtain `CommRing` / apply the ε-iso lemma at the section ring (friction a)
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no** instance that finds `CommRing` on a `forget₂ CommRingCat
31	  RingCat`-image carrier, and Mathlib does not want one — the idiom is to keep the object a
32	  `CommRingCat` (so its `CommRing` instance is the canonical one) and only forget to `RingCat`
33	  at the very last step. The relevant Mathlib facts: `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`
34	  (`Mathlib/.../ModuleCat/ChangeOfRings.lean`, the underlying map of `ε` is the ring map) and
35	  `CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso` (an iso's underlying map is bijective).
36	- **Verified by `lean_run_code`**:
37	  - `infer_instance : CommRing ↑(X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj W)` **FAILS**; so does
38	    `CommRing ↑((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj A)` for `A : CommRingCat`.
39	  - `change CommRing ↑(X.presheaf.obj W); infer_instance` **succeeds** (canonicalize to the
40	    `CommRingCat` carrier).
41	  - `((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g).hom = g.hom` is **`rfl`**, and
42	    `ModuleCat.restrictScalars ((forget₂ …).map g).hom = ModuleCat.restrictScalars g.hom` is
43	    **`rfl`**.  Since `β = whiskerRight α (forget₂ …)` with
44	    `α.app U = (f.appIso U.unop).inv` a **`CommRingCat`** morphism, `β.app U`'s restrictScalars
45	    is `rfl`-equal to `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom`.
46	  - `IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.inv.hom))` for a `CommRingCat`
47	    iso `e : A ≅ B` is provable verbatim by the `restrictScalars_isIso_ε` recipe
48	    (`bijective_of_isIso` → `RingEquiv.ofBijective` → `restrictScalars_η` → `RingEquiv.bijective`),
49	    with `CommRing ↑A`, `CommRing ↑B` found **natively** (A, B are `CommRingCat`).
50	- **Project's current path**: feed `β.app (op W')` (a `forget₂`'d `RingCat` hom) to
51	  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`, which then fails to synthesize `CommRing` on the
52	  `RingCat`-carrier spelling.
53	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (the friction is self-inflicted by forgetting to `RingCat` too
54	  early; no parallel API is needed).
55	- **Cost of divergence**: a hand-rolled "recover `CommRing` on a `forget₂` image" lemma would be
56	  a parallel API for something Mathlib deliberately routes around. The project already has the
57	  right lemma (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); it just must be applied to the
58	  `CommRingCat`-level hom.
59	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (use the `CommRingCat` iso; do **not** build a CommRing-recovery
60	  helper).
61	
62	### Decision: bridging `𝟙_ (ModuleCat S)` to the `restr`/`𝟙_X`-section unit spellings (friction b)
63	
64	- **Mathlib idiom**: there is **no** dedicated coherence lemma, and none is needed — the spellings
65	  are **definitionally equal**. The idiom is `show`/`change` to the canonical
66	  `𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(R.obj U))` form (carrier written as the **`CommRingCat`** section `↑(R.obj U)`),
67	  then `exact`.
68	- **Verified by `lean_run_code`**:
69	  - `(𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj U = 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(R.obj U))`
70	    is **`rfl`**. (`restr U N`'s section at a slice object `W` is `N.obj (op W.left)` by
71	    `pushforward₀`'s `.obj` reduction, so a `restr`/`𝟙_X`-section of the unit is just `𝟙_X`
72	    evaluated, hence the same `rfl`.)
73	  - `inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.inv.hom))` typechecks with endpoints
74	    `(restrictScalars e.inv.hom).obj (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑A)) ⟶ 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑B)` — exactly the
75	    canonical unit forms, so it unifies with the goal after canonicalization.
76	- **GOTCHA (verified)**: `MonoidalCategoryStruct (ModuleCat ↑((R ⋙ forget₂ …).obj U))` **FAILS**
77	  to synthesize even though plain `CommRing ↑((R ⋙ forget₂ …).obj U)` succeeds. So the unit must
78	  be written with the `↑(R.obj U)` (CommRingCat) carrier — at the `forget₂`-composite carrier the
79	  `𝟙_` notation itself cannot elaborate. `change`/`show` to `↑(R.obj U)` first.
80	- **Gap**: identical-up-to-defeq (no Mathlib lemma exists or is needed).
81	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (defeq via `show`/`change`; no transport lemma).
82	
83	### Decision: should the ε-iso-of-ring-equiv lemma come from Mathlib? (completeness)
84	
85	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib ships `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η` and
86	  `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv` (a `ModuleCat S ≌ ModuleCat R` for a ring
87	  equiv — a *categorical* equivalence, **not** the monoidal-unit `ε` iso). There is **no**
88	  `IsIso (ε (restrictScalars e))` / strong-monoidal-of-ring-equiv lemma in Mathlib.
89	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL — already correctly filled by the project's
90	  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε` / `_of_bijective`. No new build required.
91

96	(op W')).hom`); obtain `hbij := ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (f.appIso W').inv`; supply
97	`haveI := restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective g hbij` (CommRing on the `CommRingCat` carriers is

100	domain matches the leg-A composite. Finally `show`/`change` the goal's unit endpoints into

input.new_string:
/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/

open Opposite in
/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)

open Opposite in
/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean — fine-grained pass (iter-262)

Objective (lane 2, STUCK corrective): extract leg-B's ε-iso as a NAMED atomic lemma and close it
(the measurable reduction dissolving the STUCK watch), then fill the remaining `sliceDualTransport`
sub-holes. Recipe: `analogies/ma-legb262.md`.

## Sentence decomposition of leg-B (the dissolved frictions)

### Sentence B0: "ε of restrictScalars along the open-immersion ring iso is an iso" → `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`
- **Type:** `IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`).
  `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (f.appIso W').inv.hom (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (f.appIso W').inv)`.
  Phrased at the **CommRingCat** carrier (CommRing native), per ma-legb262 friction (a). NEW named lemma.

### Sentence B1: "the codomain unit ring swap = inv ε" → `dualUnitRingSwap`
- **Type:** `(restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom).obj (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op (f.opensFunctor.obj W'))))) ⟶ 𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. `haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'; inv (ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom))`.
  Endpoints written at the CommRingCat section carriers (ma-legb262 friction (b): the `forget₂`-composite
  carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct`). NEW named def.

### Sentence B2: "the codomainMap hole of `sliceDualTransport` is this swap" → inline fill
- **Result:** RESOLVED — replaced the `?_` at the `app` field with `dualUnitRingSwap f W.unop.left`.
  The `CommRingCat`-carrier endpoints reconcile by **`rfl`/defeq** with the goal's `restr`/`𝟙_`-section
  spellings, and `(β.app (op W')).hom = (f.appIso W').inv.hom` by `rfl` (`forget₂`-map of a CommRingCat
  hom). **No `change`/`eqToHom` bridge needed** — the analogy's defeq prediction held exactly.
  `sliceDualTransport` typed-sorry count 7 → 6.

## Remaining `sliceDualTransport` `≃ₗ`-packaging fields (typed sorries, precise blockers)

Goal order verified by `lean_goal`: naturality, map_add', map_smul', invFun, left_inv, right_inv.

### naturality (toFun) — PARTIAL
- Thin-poset square over `(Over (unop V))ᵒᵖ`. `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext` reduces it; the
  `restr`-connecting edges agree by `Subsingleton.elim`, but the `restrictScalars`-functor `.map` of the
  reindexed `φ.app` must be commuted through `dualUnitRingSwap`.
- **Blocker:** needs ε-naturality of `restrictScalars` along the structure ring iso (an `erw`-paste,
  not yet built).

### map_add' — PARTIAL (route built & probed)
- `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro W; show (restrictScalars _).map ((x+y).app _) ≫ _ = _`
  beta-reduces the LHS struct `.app W` (verified via `lean_multi_attempt`). RHS splits with
  `PresheafOfModules.add_app`.
- **Exact blocker:** the `+` on `x y : ↑(((pushforward β).obj M.val.dual).obj V)` is the
  **`internalHomObjModule` MODULE add** on the dual section object, which is NOT syntactically the
  `PresheafOfModules.Hom` add, so `PresheafOfModules.add_app` / `Functor.map_add` report "pattern not
  found" on the LHS `(x+y).app`. Needs an `internalHomObjModule`-add ↦ Hom-add bridge (defeq; a
  `change`/unfold of `internalHomObjModule`'s add field first).

### map_smul' — PARTIAL
- Identical blocker to map_add', with the `internalHomObjModule` scalar action `m • x` (and `RingHom.id`
  on the RHS scalar).

### invFun — sorry (not yet constructed)
- Mirror of `toFun` with `(f.appIso W').hom` (not `.inv`), the inverse down-set bijection
  `image_preimage_of_le`, and codomain swap `ε (restrictScalars …)` (not `inv ε`). A full
  `PresheafOfModules.Hom` build over the X-slice `Over fV`.

### left_inv / right_inv — sorry (blocked on invFun)
- `Iso.inv_hom_id` / `Iso.hom_inv_id` round-trip of `f.appIso` + the down-set bijection. Both reference
  `?refine_4` (invFun), so blocked until invFun lands.

## `dual_restrict_iso` (L416) — UNCHANGED
- Still has its `isoMk` naturality sorry (assembly residual, consumes `sliceDualTransport`). Not entered
  this pass (transitively blocked on the open `sliceDualTransport` fields). `dual_isLocallyTrivial`
  inherits it axiomatically (compiles).

## Summary
- **3/3 leg-B sentences CLOSED** (`isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`, `dualUnitRingSwap`, codomainMap fill),
  both new decls axiom-clean.
- **Sorry count:** file-level sorry-bearing declarations unchanged at 2 (`sliceDualTransport`,
  `dual_restrict_iso`), BUT `sliceDualTransport`'s internal typed-sorry holes reduced **7 → 6** and two
  axiom-clean named lemmas added. The STUCK watch's blocker (leg-B ε-iso) is dissolved as predicted by
  ma-legb262 — including the friction-(b) defeq reconciliation, which needed NO bridge.
- 2 map-law fields (map_add'/map_smul') are PARTIAL with the exact blocker isolated (the
  internalHomObjModule-add vs Hom-add syntactic mismatch); naturality + invFun + the two round-trips
  remain.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** leg-B fully closed as 2 named axiom-clean decls + codomainMap defeq-filled
  (measurable: 7→6 holes in `sliceDualTransport`). This was the stated BAR for this lane and it is met;
  the STUCK watch is dissolved.
- **Partial progress:** map_add'/map_smul' — route to the sectionwise equation is built (hom_ext +
  `show` beta-reduction + RHS `add_app` split), blocked at a single, precisely-identified structural
  friction: the dual section object's module `+`/`•` (from `internalHomObjModule`) is not syntactically
  the `PresheafOfModules.Hom` add/smul, so `add_app`/`Functor.map_add` don't fire. Next step: add a small
  `internalHomObjModule`-add↦Hom-add (and -smul) defeq bridge lemma (likely a `change` + unfold of the
  `internalHomObjModule` add field), then `Functor.map_add, add_comp` close it.
- **Not attempted in depth:** invFun (reverse construction, ~mirror of toFun) and its left_inv/right_inv
  — these are the remaining genuine build (the planner-noted ~150-250 LOC). invFun must be built before
  left_inv/right_inv are reachable.

## Markers ready (for review agent / sync_leanok)
- `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`, `dualUnitRingSwap` — fully proved, axiom-clean → eligible for
  `\leanok` on their blueprint statement+proof blocks (`lem:slice_dual_transport` leg-B sub-claims in
  `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, if/where the writer added `\lean{dualUnitRingSwap}` hints).
- `sliceDualTransport`, `dual_restrict_iso` — still contain sorries → NOT `\leanok`.

## Housekeeping done
- Updated the stale in-file header STATUS NOTE (item 1b, ~L24–35): route-1-DEAD + route-2-leg-B-RESOLVED
  (aud261/aud259 majors).
- Rewrote the `sliceDualTransport` body bullet comments with accurate per-field labels (matching the
